commitResults.messageBody: Add TransactionManager CRUD operations and ModifyCommand
Add TransactionManager CRUD operations and ModifyCommand

- Implement BudgetCommand with set/view/edit/delete functionality
- Add BudgetManager to handle budget data and progress tracking
- Create MonthlyBudget class for per-month budget storage
- Define 12 budget categories (Food to Savings)
- Integrate with TransactionManager to update budgets automatically
- Add budget progress bars and formatted displays
- Connect income transactions to disposable income calculation

- Implement BudgetCommand with set/view/edit/delete functionality

- Create MonthlyBudget class for per-month budget storage
- Define 12 budget categories (Food to Savings)

- Add budget progress bars and formatted displays
- Connect income transactions to disposable income calculation

commitResults.messageTitle: 1. Amended 4.7 of UG to reflect app's actual behaviour (Fixes #94) 2. Amended 7.2 table 2 - adding a transaction of more than 2dp will save amount to nearest 2dp (Fixes #104) 3. `ModifyCommand.java` - Fixes #81 (multiple fields with spaces) and #85 (consistent error messages) 4. `AddCommand.java` - Fixes #85 (consistent errors), #88 (multi-word categories), #98 (unclosed quote detection) 5. `DeleteCommand.java` - Fixes #85 (consistent error messages) 6. `BudgetManager.java` - Fixes #92 (threshold re-firing) and #93 (multi-word category matching). Prevent repeated warning notifications when approaching budget allocation 7. `BudgetCommand.java` - Fixes #100 (prevent overwrite) and #111 (TOTAL row) 8. `FilterCommand.java` - Update for category code support

Fix NaN and Infinity bypassing amount validation
commitResults.messageBody:
Double.parseDouble() accepts "NaN" and "Infinity" as valid inputs,
which silently passed the <= 0 and > MAX_AMOUNT checks. Added explicit
guards in AddCommand and ModifyCommand to reject these values.

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix NaN and Infinity bypassing amount validation
commitResults.messageBody:
Double.parseDouble() accepts "NaN" and "Infinity" as valid inputs,
which silently passed the <= 0 and > MAX_AMOUNT checks. Added explicit
guards in AddCommand and ModifyCommand to reject these values.
